title: Named Structs
description: Treat structs as reusable Custom Data Types.

slides.title: Named Structures 📛
slides.content:
By giving a name to the struct, you can treat it as a data type.

This means that you can create variables with this structure anywhere in the program at any time.

slides.title: Creating a Blueprint
slides.content: To create a named structure, put the name of the structure right after the `struct` keyword.

quizData.question: What is the primary benefit of a 'named structure' like `struct car`?

options.text: It runs faster than an unnamed structure
It acts as a reusable blueprint, allowing you to instantiate multiple 'car' objects easily
It automatically saves data to the hard drive

quizData.explanation: Named structs are essentially the predecessor to modern 'Classes' in Object Oriented Programming. They act as a blueprint.

slides.title: Best Practices 👍
slides.content: Just like standard functions, named `struct` definitions should be placed outside and *above* the `main()` function so that `main()` can read the blueprint before running.

quizData.question: When defining a named struct, like `struct car { ... };`, you MUST end the block with a semicolon.
quizData.explanation: Yes! Unlike functions, struct block definitions firmly require a terminating semicolon `};` at the end.
